ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Alla kontrollpunkter stämmer överens mellan certifikatet och fakturan. Avsändaren Korab International AB i Sverige, mottagaren Jamana Printers Limited i Tanzania, varubeskrivningen 756 Reels Newsprint, kvantiteten 496.616 MT och ursprungslandet Norge kan alla verifieras i fakturan. Dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.summary: Certificate of Origin SEG-23D-788990 verified against invoice 51572. All five checkpoints (consignor, consignee, goods description, quantity, country of origin) result in MATCH. Full alignment between certificate and invoice.

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF. All fields readable. Standard ICC Certificate of Origin format with bilingual headers.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF. All fields readable. Single page commercial invoice with complete details.

lc_check.lc_verification_note: No Letter of Credit reference found in certificate. The certificate number SEG-23D-788990 does not contain an LC-related bank term designation. Remarks section contains Order no: 28750 and Invoice no: 51572 — no LC reference.

locations_found: Invoice header/branding: 'Korab International AB' with logo at top of page
Invoice issuer address block: 'Korab International AB, Mårten Krakowgatan 2, 411 04 Göteborg, Sweden'
VAT NO: SE556564039701 (SE prefix confirms Sweden)

criteria_met: Criterion 1: Certificate invoice reference 51572 matches invoice number 51572
Criterion 2: Certificate consignor 'Korab International AB' matches invoice issuer 'Korab International AB'
Criterion 3: Certificate consignee 'Jamana Printers Limited' matches invoice SOLD TO 'Jamana Printers Limited'
pairing_validation.note: All three pairing criteria are satisfied. Document pair is valid.

checkpoints.certificate_value: Korab International AB, Mårten Krakowgatan 2, SE411 04, GÖTEBORG, Sweden
checkpoints.invoice_value: Korab International AB, Mårten Krakowgatan 2, 411 04 Göteborg, Sweden

comparison_steps.description: Search for certificate consignor name 'Korab International AB' in invoice
comparison_steps.result: FOUND – Name appears in invoice header, issuer address block, and footer/signature block. Direct match as invoice issuer.

comparison_steps.description: Invoice issuer identified as 'Korab International AB' in header block with address 'Mårten Krakowgatan 2, 411 04 Göteborg, Sweden'

comparison_steps.step: 4.1.2 – Verification of company name and country
comparison_steps.description: Company name: Certificate 'Korab International AB' vs Invoice 'Korab International AB' — exact match. Country: Certificate 'Sweden' vs Invoice 'Sweden' (from address). Additionally confirmed by VAT NO SE556564039701 (SE prefix = Sweden per rule 4.1.2.1).
Certificate country 'Sweden' matches invoice country 'Sweden'. Also corroborated by VAT prefix 'SE' and IBAN prefix 'SE'.

rules_applied.description: Corporate structure – consignor name found directly in invoice as invoice issuer
Verification of company name and country
Country verification via VAT prefix SE confirming Sweden

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor 'Korab International AB' at 'Mårten Krakowgatan 2, SE411 04, GÖTEBORG, Sweden' was verified against invoice issuer 'Korab International AB' at 'Mårten Krakowgatan 2, 411 04 Göteborg, Sweden'. Company name is an exact match. Country Sweden is confirmed by explicit address, VAT prefix SE, and IBAN prefix SE. Minor address formatting differences (SE prefix on postcode in certificate vs plain postcode in invoice; GÖTEBORG vs Göteborg) are non-material after normalization. Result: MATCH per rules 4.1.0, 4.1.2, 4.1.4.

checkpoints.certificate_value: Jamana Printers Limited, Plot No. 9 Nyerere Road, Pugu Road, P.O Box 5584, Dar es Salaam, Tanzania
checkpoints.invoice_value: Jamana Printers Limited, Plot No. 9 Nyerere Road, Pugu Road, P.O Box 5584, Dar es Salaam, Tanzania

comparison_steps.description: Invoice contains a primary billing field 'SOLD TO' with named party 'Jamana Printers Limited'. This is the authoritative consignee per priority rules.
Certificate consignee 'Jamana Printers Limited' vs Invoice SOLD TO 'Jamana Printers Limited' — exact match after normalization.
Certificate consignee country 'Tanzania' vs Invoice SOLD TO country 'Tanzania' — exact match.

rules_applied.description: Priority identification – SOLD TO field is primary billing field
Verification of company name – exact match

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee 'Jamana Printers Limited' at 'Plot No. 9 Nyerere Road, Pugu Road, P.O Box 5584, Dar es Salaam, Tanzania' was verified against invoice SOLD TO field 'Jamana Printers Limited' at 'Plot No. 9 Nyerere Road, Pugu Road, P.O Box 5584, Dar es Salaam, Tanzania'. Company name and full address are exact matches. Country Tanzania matches exactly. Result: MATCH per rules 4.2.0.2, 4.2.2, 4.2.3.

comparison_steps.description: No article numbers are specified in the certificate. Priority 1 is not applicable. Proceeding to Priority 2.

Identifying the identity-bearing main designation in the certificate: 'Newsprint'. The certificate also specifies '756 Reels' as packaging/quantity description. Searching for 'Newsprint' in invoice description: found as '756 Reels: Newsprint'. The identity-bearing main designation 'Newsprint' is exactly present in the invoice after normalization (case-insensitive). The quantity descriptor '756 Reels' also matches exactly.

Invoice contains additional specifications (Substance: 42,5G, dimensions 840mm/980mm, Diameter: 1250mm, Core: 76mm) that are not present in certificate. Per rule 4.3.4, these are non-identity-bearing additions that do not alter the product identity. The main designation 'Newsprint' is identifiable in both documents. No MISMATCH triggered.

rules_applied.description: Controlled text matching – identity-bearing main designation 'Newsprint' identified exactly in invoice
Non-identity-bearing additions in invoice (substance, dimensions) do not affect product identity

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate goods description '756 Reels / Newsprint' was verified against invoice description '756 Reels: Newsprint'. No article numbers in certificate, so Priority 2 (controlled text matching) applies. Identity-bearing main designation 'Newsprint' is exactly present in invoice. Quantity descriptor '756 Reels' matches exactly. Invoice contains additional specifications (substance, dimensions, core) which are non-identity-bearing additions per rule 4.3.4. Result: MATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate box 7 states '496.616 MT'. Numeric value: 496.616. Unit: MT (metric tons). Both numeric value and unit are explicitly present. No explicit weight category (GW/NW) is stated in the certificate quantity field.

Box 7 contains '496.616 MT'. This is the verification-basis quantity. Box 6 also contains '756 Reels' as a packaging/quantity descriptor. Both are valid quantity indicators, but box 7 is the primary verification basis per rule 4.4.2.1.
comparison_steps.result: 496.616 MT from box 7 is the primary verification quantity

comparison_steps.description: Searching for 496.616 MT in invoice. Invoice TOTAL NET WEIGHT field states '496.616 MT'. Exact numeric value match: 496.616 = 496.616. Unit MT matches MT. MATCH found directly.

Certificate states '496.616 MT' without explicit GW/NW category. Invoice has TOTAL NET WEIGHT: 496.616 MT and TOTAL GROSS WEIGHT: 496,616 KG. The value 496.616 matches the NW field exactly. Applying rule 4.4.2.2: certificate has one value (496.616 MT) without category. The TOTAL GROSS WEIGHT '496,616 KG' — interpreting comma as thousands separator gives 496616 KG = 496.616 MT, which is numerically identical to the NW. Both GW and NW are the same numeric value (496.616 MT = 496,616 KG). Since the certificate value matches the NW field exactly and the GW field (after unit conversion) is the same value, there is no contradicting alternative. Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2: single value, matches invoice, no contradicting alternative weight → MATCH.

Certificate also contains '756 Reels' in box 6. Invoice description states '756 Reels: Newsprint'. This secondary quantity also matches. Primary quantity (496.616 MT) already verified as MATCH. No contradicting quantities in invoice.

rules_applied.description: Occurrence check – 496.616 MT found exactly in invoice TOTAL NET WEIGHT field
Box 7 value (496.616 MT) used as verification-basis quantity
Weight category – single value without explicit category, matches invoice NW exactly, no contradicting alternative
Multiple quantities – secondary quantity 756 Reels also consistent

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate quantity '496.616 MT' from box 7 was verified against invoice. Invoice TOTAL NET WEIGHT field states '496.616 MT' — exact numeric and unit match. Certificate does not specify GW or NW explicitly, but the value matches the NW field exactly. Invoice also states TOTAL GROSS WEIGHT: 496,616 KG — which, interpreting the comma as thousands separator, equals 496616 KG = 496.616 MT, the same value. Since both weight fields in the invoice have the same numeric value, there is no ambiguity about which category the certificate's value corresponds to. Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2: single value, matched in invoice, no contradicting alternative → MATCH. Additionally, the secondary quantity '756 Reels' from box 6 is consistent with invoice description '756 Reels: Newsprint'. No contradictions found. Result: MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH due to missing weight category (GW/NW) in certificate
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Rule 4.4.2.2 point 2 applies: certificate states a single weight value without category, this value can be identified in the invoice (TOTAL NET WEIGHT: 496.616 MT), and the invoice does not contain a contradicting alternative weight value of a different category (GW and NW happen to be the same numeric value). Therefore MATCH, not MISMATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Invoice contains explicit field 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: Norway'. This is a dedicated origin field.
Certificate: 'Norway'. Invoice: 'Norway'. Exact match, no normalization required.
Invoice also contains statement 'these goods are of Norwegian origin' in certification text at bottom. This further corroborates Norway as origin.

rules_applied.description: Origin identified in explicit COUNTRY OF ORIGIN field in invoice
Normalization – exact match, no normalization needed
MATCH – origin Norway explicitly identified in invoice

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate country of origin 'Norway' (box 3) was verified against invoice field 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: Norway'. Exact match. Additionally corroborated by invoice certification statement 'these goods are of Norwegian origin'. Note: consignor is based in Sweden while origin is Norway — this is entirely normal per rule 4.5 (origin refers to where goods are produced, not where the consignor is established). Result: MATCH.

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: NOT_APPLICABLE – No Letter of Credit reference found in certificate
rule_evaluation_log.details: Certificate remarks contain Order no: 28750 and Invoice no: 51572. Certificate number is SEG-23D-788990. None of these constitute an LC reference with bank term context.

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: PASS – Two files received, Document A is certificate, Document B is invoice
rule_evaluation_log.details: Certificate file contains one certificate page. Invoice file contains one invoice page. No embedded invoices in certificate file. Standard pairing.
Criterion 1: Invoice reference 51572 matches. Criterion 2: Consignor 'Korab International AB' matches invoice issuer. Criterion 3: Consignee 'Jamana Printers Limited' matches invoice SOLD TO.
Exact name and country match. Korab International AB / Sweden in both documents.
Exact name and country match. Jamana Printers Limited / Tanzania in both documents.
Identity-bearing main designation 'Newsprint' and quantity descriptor '756 Reels' both found exactly in invoice. No article numbers in certificate.
496.616 MT found exactly in invoice TOTAL NET WEIGHT field. GW and NW are same numeric value in this case.
Norway found exactly in invoice COUNTRY OF ORIGIN field. Additionally corroborated by origin statement in invoice text.

human_readable_report.conclusion: All five verification checkpoints (consignor, consignee, goods description, quantity, country of origin) result in MATCH. The certificate is verified as IDENTICAL to the invoice. Eligible for automatic approval.

output_quality_checks.consistency_details: All 5 checkpoints are MATCH → comparison_result is IDENTICAL. No MISMATCH or MANUAL_REVIEW checkpoints. Workflow recommendation is AUTO_APPROVAL_ELIGIBLE, consistent with IDENTICAL result. All confidence values ≥ 0.85. No contradictions between motivations and statuses.
output_quality_checks.confidence_floor_details: All applicable checkpoints have confidence ≥ 0.97, well above the 0.85 floor for IDENTICAL.
